arts.ttl: Manchester United serious transfer interest in elite European forward as Ruben Amorim seeks reinforcements
arts.sum: Manchester United reportedly sent scouts to watch Eintracht Frankfurt's Omar Marmoush on Thursday and he is of serious interest to Ruben Amorim and the United hierarchy.
Match of the Day 2 pundits Alan Shearer and David Moyes analyse how Manchester United capitalised on Everton's lack of end product in the final third.

arts.ttl: Man Utd 'far from perfect' in win over Everton - Amorim
arts.sum: Manchester United boss Ruben Amorim says his side's emphatic victory over Everton was "far from perfect" despite the one-sided scoreline.

arts.ttl: Man United fans think Ruben Amorim threw shade at former manager Erik ten Hag with comments about star after Everton thrashing
arts.sum: Manchester United supporters think Ruben Amorim may have made a subtle dig at former manager Erik ten Hag with one of his post-match comments on Sunday.

arts.ttl: 'It's just madness' - Dyche perplexed by Everton defending
arts.sum: Everton manager Sean Dyche says his side's defending for Manchester United's third goal was "madness" in their 4-0 loss at Old Trafford.

arts.ttl: Revealed: Chelsea have 'concrete interest' in key Man Utd target
arts.sum: Chelsea reportedly hold “concrete interest” in Sporting CP wonderkid Geovany Quenda, who has been heavily linked with a move to Manchester United in recent weeks. Quenda’s current release clause at Sporting CP stands at €100 million, but Man Utd are preparing a €60m offer for the 17-year-old, as per Portuguese outlet A Bola .

arts.ttl: Roy Keane spots big boost for Man United just three games into Ruben Amorim era
arts.sum: Manchester United scored four as they defeated Everton comfortably at Old Trafford in the Premier League on Sunday afternoon

arts.ttl: Jadon Sancho shows true Manchester United colours after Everton victory
arts.sum: Manchester United picked up a big victory in Ruben Amorim's first home Premier League match as Everton fell to a 4-0 defeat

arts.ttl: Manchester United fans protest 'unethical' increase in ticket prices – video
arts.sum: Hundreds of Manchester United fans gathered in front of Old Trafford to protest against a recent increase in matchday ticket prices. Prices, which previously started at £40 for adults and £25 for children, were raised to £66 per game, with no concessions for children or pensioners. The move comes as part of the co-owner Sir Jim Ratcliffe’s efforts to make United more financially sustainable in the face of losses. Fans outside the ground called the move 'unethical' and directed a number of chants at the ownership. Some Everton supporters also joined their United counterparts while Manchester City and Liverpool fans staged their own protest at Anfield in solidarity to support the Football Supporters' Association's 'Stop Exploiting Loyalty' campaign. Manchester United raise member ticket prices to £66 and remove concessions Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Man Utd Have 'No Interest' in Letting 'Elite' Star Leave in January
arts.sum: Joshua Zirkzee has finally burst into life at Manchester United after a slow start at Old Trafford
